key_files.relevance: Core media rendering component - handles both outgoing (local blobs) and incoming (remote URLs) attachments, including image display, blurhash placeholders, lightbox
Parses mediaJson from database into renderable attachments, handles both outgoing format ({attachments}) and incoming format ({tags}), provides NIP-94 imeta and NIP-92 url tag parsing
Decrypts NIP-17 messages, currently only accepts kind 14, returns tags array from unwrapped rumor. CRITICAL CHANGE POINT: must extend to accept kind 15
Builds incoming mediaJson from rumor tags (imeta/url) during message ingestion, stores in nostr_messages.media_json column. EXTENSION POINT: handle kind 15 tags
Fetches and caches remote images from URLs, uses IPC to interact with image-cache service, provides fallback handling
Parses message content to detect URLs, splits into text and link segments. EXTENSION POINT: detect image URLs for inline rendering
MessageContent and MessageBubble components - MessageContent currently renders all URLs as text links. EXTENSION POINT: inline image rendering
SUPPORTED_MIME_TYPES for outgoing attachments. NO CHANGES needed (outgoing only)
Main process entry point orchestrating database, services, IPC registration. Shows overall architecture
Security boundary exposing typed IPC API via contextBridge. Shows renderer-main communication pattern
Dual-instance test procedures T01-T12. CRITICAL: All behavioral changes must be verified here
Dual-instance environment setup with Playwright MCP. CRITICAL: Test design principles - UI interactions only, no IPC for actions
Example media upload E2E test showing pattern: store blob → send message → wait for upload → verify rendering
E2E test helpers: ensureIdentityExists, setupBlossomServer, waitForMessageSent, waitForMediaUploadComplete

patterns.name: Dual-format media storage
patterns.description: parseMediaJson() detects format via parsed.tags vs parsed.attachments presence, calls format-specific parser

patterns.name: IPC-based resource resolution
patterns.description: Components use window.api.* calls for blob storage and image cache, handle async resolution in useEffect with cancellation

patterns.name: Conditional rendering by MIME
patterns.description: isImageMimeType() gates ImageAttachment vs FileAttachment rendering

patterns.name: Blurhash placeholder
patterns.description: Show blurhash canvas when displayUrl missing but blurhash present, provides visual feedback during load

patterns.name: Tag-to-mediaJson transformation
patterns.description: Incoming rumor tags filtered for imeta/url, stored as {tags: [...]} in media_json column

patterns.name: E2E data isolation
patterns.description: Per-test unique data directories prevent identity/subscription accumulation across tests

patterns.name: IPC polling for async operations
patterns.description: waitForMessageSent, waitForMediaUploadComplete poll until desired state reached

patterns.name: Dual-instance UI-only testing
patterns.description: Test procedures execute all actions via UI interactions (click, type, drag), never via IPC calls

similar_features.name: Media attachment rendering (incoming)
similar_features.description: Renders images and files from mediaJson, already handles incoming imeta tags format

similar_features.name: CachedImage remote URL fetching
similar_features.description: Fetches remote images via IPC image cache, provides fallback handling - can be reused for inline images

similar_features.name: NIP-94 imeta tag parsing
similar_features.description: Parses NIP-94 imeta tags into ParsedMediaAttachment structure - can be extended for kind 15 tags

architecture.process_model: Electron three-process: Main (Node.js), Preload (security boundary), Renderer (sandboxed Chromium)

module_boundaries.main: crypto, service, blob-storage, database - owns all filesystem, network, crypto
module_boundaries.preload: IPC facade via contextBridge - enforces contextIsolation=true
module_boundaries.renderer: React components, utils - zero direct access to Node APIs

architecture.data_flow: Encrypted event → decryptNip17Message → ingestIncomingMessage → database (media_json column) → renderer parseMediaJson → MediaAttachments
key_abstraction_layers: Crypto layer: encryptNip17Message/decryptNip17Message - operates on Uint8Array, never exposes keys
Service layer: NostlingService - coordinates crypto, relay pool, database
Database layer: SQLite with migrations - media_blobs table, nostr_messages.media_json column
Renderer layer: MediaAttachments component - normalizes both formats into ParsedMediaAttachment[]

extension_points.change: Change if (!rumor || rumor.kind !== 14) to accept kind 14 and 15
extension_points.impact: Allows kind-15 file messages to pass through decryption

extension_points.change: Extend tag parsing to handle kind-15 specific tags: file-type, dim, blurhash, x, thumb
Add MIME inference from URL extension when m field missing
extension_points.impact: Handles incoming attachments without explicit MIME type

extension_points.change: No changes needed - already accepts any image/* MIME
extension_points.impact: Extended formats (AVIF, BMP, SVG) already supported

extension_points.change: Detect image URLs in message text, render as CachedImage instead of text link
extension_points.impact: Inline image rendering from URLs in message content

e2e_tests.config: Single worker (sequential), 30s timeout, trace on retry, screenshot on failure
e2e_tests.run: npm run test:e2e:docker or make test-e2e-serial (REQUIRED)

dual_instance.verification: browser_take_screenshot, browser_evaluate (NOT browser_snapshot - unreliable with Chakra UI)
dual_instance.test_design_principle: All actions via UI interactions (click, type, drag), NEVER via IPC for actions

conventions.rule: E2E tests MUST use npm run test:e2e:docker to avoid desktop system interference
All behavioral changes MUST be verified before and after in UI using dual-instance test environment
Production code MUST NOT contain adaptations or tweaks necessary only for tests
Use browser_take_screenshot or browser_evaluate for verification; browser_snapshot unreliable with Chakra UI
Cleanup temporary files after implementation (markdown files, code backups)
MIME type field naming: mimeType in attachments, m in imeta tags, file-type in kind-15 tags
MediaJson backward compatibility: Parser must handle both {attachments: [...]} and {tags: [...]} formats
